home.mathBattles: Where sharp minds collide and collaboration wins — register for Almaty Math Battles!
home.kids: Children from various backgrounds from all over Kazakhstan gather here in Almaty to share the joy of math in a challenging and fun competition.
home.onlineParticipants: online participants in the last tournament
home.offlineParticipants: offline participants in the last tournament
home.fund: total prize money awarded to winners
home.republicanOlympiadParticipants: National Math Olympiad medalists among our participants
home.republicanOlympiadJury: National Math Olympiad medalists among our adjudicators
home.about: Almaty Math Battles 2026 - registration is open!
home.ourMissionContext: Our aim is to spread the joy of learning new things and solving complicated problems with as many people as possible by organizing tournaments, camps, online and offline courses and lessons that are made available to everyone interested. We gather people from all over Kazakhstan to promote the exchange of ideas and foster a passion for mathematics through friendly competition. Our events are just as enriching for our team members and we welcome all interested mathematicians to join us.
home.registerContext: <link>Registration</link> for the qualifying round is open until July 2, 8:00 PM Almaty time (GMT +5).
home.onlineContext:
The online selection will take place on July 3, 2026, on the AutoProctor platform from 10:00 AM to 12:45 PM Almaty time (GMT +5). 
 All registered participants will receive platform access instructions closer to the date.
home.finalContext: The final in-person round will take place from August 3 to 7 at the International University of Information Technology in Almaty, Republic of Kazakhstan.

our-team.weAre: We are the Maths Battles Community, a large nonprofit tournament organization.
our-team.primaryGoal: Our primary goal is to develop teamwork and collaboration among students passionate about mathematics.
our-team.popularizationContext: Promoting the Olympiads movement among school students as a means of nurturing the intellectual elite of the country and encourage people from underprivileged backgrounds
our-team.collaborationContext: Supporting interactions and experience exchange among the country's brightest minds through teamwork and cooperation within the teams, organizers and volunteers
our-team.funContext: Our Math Battles are always a stressful event - olympiads always are! But out team does their best to moderate the experience and make sure the competition is fun and fair for everyone.

gallery.selection: A selection of photos from our events.

registration.registration: Registration Form for Almaty Maths Battles 2026
registration.registrationContext: Before filling out this form, make sure you've read and understood the <link>rules of the tournament</link>. You can edit your response after filling out the form via a letter from Google Forms that will be sent to your email. Please note that teams must consist of either 4 or 5 participants.

registerform.languageHint: All team members must be fluent in the selected participation language.
registerform.teamLeaderDescription: Team leader is the person responsible for the team. Cannot be a team member or minor. Must be an adult with a notarized power of attorney from parents of minor participants. Can be a teacher, school employee, or any authorized adult. Required for all teams.
registerform.rulesConfirm: By filling out this form, team members confirm they have read and understood
registerform.rulesLink: the main regulations of Almaty Math Battles
registerform.dataProcessingConfirm: I consent to the collection, processing and storage of Personal Data in accordance with Schedule №03-08/05.
registerform.dataProcessingLink: the Foundation's data processing policy and schedule
registerform.dataProcessingConfirmSuffix: can be found on the Foundation website.
registerform.text1: the Fund's personal data policy and list
registerform.text3: the Fund's personal data policy and list
registerform.text4: can be found on the Fund's website
registerform.submittedDescription: To complete registration, the parent/guardian or authorized representative of each participant must complete a separate consent form on AutoProctor.
registerform.submittedHint: Forward the link below and remind them to enter your phone number or email — this is how we'll link the forms in our database:

files.name: AMB 2025 Baiterek
files.note: Some of the problems were proposed by <link>Aleph</link>

notFound.missingContext: Sorry, we can't find that page.

parentConsent.title: Parent Consent Form
parentConsent.childIdentifierHint: Enter the phone number or email that the child provided when registering their team — we'll use it to link the forms in our database.
parentConsent.isRepresentativeHint: Check this box if you are an escort or teacher with a notarized power of attorney
parentConsent.autoProcorConsent: I confirm consent to use the AutoProctor system and to process my child's associated personal data.
parentConsent.dataProcessingConsent: I consent to the collection, processing and storage of my personal data in accordance with the tournament regulations.
parentConsent.fundDataProcessingConsent: I consent to the collection, processing and storage of Personal Data in accordance with Schedule №03-08/03.
parentConsent.fundDataProcessingLink: the Foundation's data processing policy and schedule
parentConsent.fundDataProcessingConfirmSuffix: can be found on the Foundation website.
parentConsent.successMessage: Consent successfully submitted and linked to the participant!
